description_long: This object is a hand axe featuring a straight, cylindrical wooden handle and a large, rectangular metal blade. The blade is dark, likely forged from steel or iron, and is attached perpendicularly to the handle with a robust, blocky mounting. The handle is a deep brown, suggesting it is made from stained or aged hardwood, and appears smooth with no visible grip enhancements. The blade has a single, straight cutting edge and a flat poll on the opposite side, giving it a utilitarian appearance suitable for chopping or splitting tasks. There are no visible decorative elements, engravings, or branding on either the handle or the blade. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of axes found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds.
description: A utilitarian hand axe with a straight wooden handle and a large, rectangular dark metal blade, simply mounted and unadorned.
description_view_0: The axe is shown from the side, clearly displaying the full rectangular blade and the straight wooden handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the top, showing the narrow edge of the blade and the circular cross-section of the handle.
description_view_2: The opposite side view reveals the other face of the blade and handle, mirroring the first side.
description_view_3: The axe is seen from the bottom, highlighting the pointed end of the blade and the handle's end.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, smooth surface, no visible grip features, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Large rectangular single-edged blade, flat poll, no beard, no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted perpendicularly onto the handle.
